Browse by flow

Switch interview mode

3 flows
High-level design interview prep HLD
Architecture, distributed systems trade-offs, and end-to-end system walkthroughs for classic system design rounds.
Low-level design interview prep LLD
Object modeling, extensible APIs, machine-coding structure, and follow-up handling for class-design rounds.
Data structures and algorithms interview prep DSA
Curated coding-round practice across general problem sets and company-specific question banks for array, tree, graph, DP, and mock-round prep.

DSA learning path

Company-specific DSA rounds

Practice question clusters that resemble Amazon, Google, and other large-company coding loops, using the company datasets directly.

Module progress 0%

0 of 3 lessons complete

Next up Lesson 1: Amazon online assessment practice set

Work through Amazon-style arrays, strings, trees, and DP prompts with an emphasis on clarity and correctness under time pressure.

Why this module matters Choose the solving pattern early, then defend the invariant while you code.

Move through the lessons in order so pattern recognition, live coding structure, and company-specific reps build on each other instead of staying disconnected.

Study target 3 lessons left

Aim to finish one lesson, then explain its core trade-off out loud before moving on.

Data structures and algorithms interview prep 3 lessons 0/3 complete Follow in order for best results Back to DSA roadmap Start module View lesson runway

Learning rhythm

Study this like a sequenced course, not a reference dump

  1. Preview the runway. Skim every lesson title first so you can see the progression before you dive in.
  2. Do one lesson deeply. Read the prompt shape, pick the target pattern, and name the invariant before typing the first line of code.
  3. Say it back aloud. Explain the idea in interviewer language before you mark it complete.
  4. Only then advance. Move to the next lesson when you can defend the previous one without the notes open.
DSA sequence Designed for interview recall

Outcomes

What you should walk away with

  • Get comfortable switching between general patterns and company-specific question mixes
  • Rehearse explaining trade-offs in the style commonly expected in major tech interviews
  • Use company sets to simulate tighter, more targeted mock rounds

Practice flow

How to study each lesson

  • Read the summary and explain the core pattern or invariant in your own words.
  • Walk one example manually, then code the cleanest correct version before optimizing.
  • Answer the interview prompts aloud before marking the lesson complete.

Mastery check

How you know it is sticking

  • Clear problem restatement, complexity target, and solving pattern.
  • A stable invariant or recurrence that survives interviewer follow-ups.
  • Confident edge-case testing and clean explanation while coding.

Lesson runway

Follow the sequence from first principles to fluency

This is the core pattern borrowed from stronger learning products: show the path, make the next step obvious, and keep outcomes visible while the learner moves.

Google phone and onsite practice set

Rehearse the kind of DSA prompts and follow-up depth that show up in Google-style phone screens and onsite loops.

Microsoft, Meta, and Apple practice mix

Use a mixed-company set to simulate rounds where the prompt is less predictable and the company signal comes from the style of follow-up questions.

30-45 min 3 prompts 3 sections

Lesson 1

Amazon online assessment practice set

Open

Work through Amazon-style arrays, strings, trees, and DP prompts with an emphasis on clarity and correctness under time pressure.

Why it matters

Amazon screens frequently reward clean implementation, pragmatic edge-case handling, and quick selection of a standard pattern without overengineering.

What you will cover

Pattern lensPractice setExecution focus

Good interview signal

  • Clarify constraints, input shape, and expected output before coding.
  • Choose the intended pattern and defend its complexity.
30-45 min 3 prompts 3 sections
Start lesson

Lesson 2

Google phone and onsite practice set

Open

Rehearse the kind of DSA prompts and follow-up depth that show up in Google-style phone screens and onsite loops.

Why it matters

Google rounds often probe the reasoning behind the solution as much as the implementation. Strong answers make the invariant, recurrence, or graph state explicit before coding.

What you will cover

Pattern lensPractice setExecution focus

Good interview signal

  • Clarify constraints, input shape, and expected output before coding.
  • Choose the intended pattern and defend its complexity.
30-45 min 3 prompts 3 sections
Start lesson

Lesson 3

Microsoft, Meta, and Apple practice mix

Open

Use a mixed-company set to simulate rounds where the prompt is less predictable and the company signal comes from the style of follow-up questions.

Why it matters

Mixed-company practice is useful when you need broader DSA readiness instead of optimizing only for one interview format.

What you will cover

Pattern lensPractice setExecution focus

Good interview signal

  • Clarify constraints, input shape, and expected output before coding.
  • Choose the intended pattern and defend its complexity.
